title: Silver Speeder

{{Quote|A late entry! Boba Fett in his ''Silver Speeder''!|Announcement during the Boonta Speeder Race|<ref name="ARttF" />}}
The '''''Silver Speeder''''' was a modified [[zZip Product Concepts Limited]] [[X-X landspeeder]] owned by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Fett acquired the [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] after chasing down a [[Unidentified Iotran|mark]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rivvidu]] and equipped it well to enable him to attack his targets with an armament that included [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]] [[Power harpoon launcher|harpoons]] and [[vibrosaw]]s. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], Fett, acting on a contract from the [[Annoo-dat Blue|Annoo-dat]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Sise Fromm]] to capture the young speeder racers [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]], and their friend [[Kea Moll]], followed Joben's speeder, the ''[[White Witch]]'', into the [[Boonta Speeder Race]]. Fett attacked Joben throughout the competition, unaware that Fromm's son, [[Tig Fromm]], had planted a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] on the ''White Witch''. An attack involving his magnetic beams accidentally loosened the detonator, which attached itself to the ''Silver Speeder''. Although Fett ejected in time, the ''Silver Speeder'' was destroyed.

==Description==
A modified [[zZip Product Concepts Limited]] [[X-X landspeeder]],<ref name="P170" /> the ''Silver Speeder'' was a sleek,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|152|''Silver Speeder''}}</ref> powerful [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] which was capable of traveling at a maximum speed of 390 [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]] at up to three [[Meter/Legends|meters]] above ground level. The speeder was 3.7 meters long and had a blocky, angular fuselage with angled sections attached to either side and a large spoiler mounted at the rear, above the [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. The ''Silver Speeder'' failed to live up to its name after it came into the possession of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who repainted in subdued shades of mostly [[Color/Legends|gray]] and green to make it less conspicuous.<ref name="P170" />

[[File:SilverSpeeder-ARttF.png|thumb|left|250px|''Silver Speeder'']]
The enclosed [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] had room for a single passenger in addition to the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]. A large window to the front and small windows on either side allowed the pilot to see out of the vehicle.<ref name="P170" /> The speeder was controlled by a yoke in the cockpit, and the dashboard featured numerous controls, including those for the weapon systems, as well as a [[Computer monitor/Legends|monitor screen]].<ref name="ARttF" /> The ''Silver Speeder'' had additional space for up to ten [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] of cargo.<ref name="P170" />

Fett outfitted the ''Silver Speeder'' with several nonstandard components.<ref name="CSWE" /> A pair<ref name="ARttF" /> of [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]] [[Power harpoon launcher|harpoon]]<ref name="P170" /> beams were emitted from yellow protrusions at the front of the side sections and could be used to ensnare similar sized vehicles<ref name="ARttF" /> at a range of up to ten meters.<ref name="P170" /> Above these were emitters for a pair of [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]<ref name="ARttF" /> with a range of twenty meters<ref name="P170" /> and two grappling cables.<ref name="ARttF" /> Three [[vibrosaw]]s were mounted to the front, left and right of the vehicle to attack targets within one meter.<ref name="P170" />

==History==
{{Quote|Your meddling has cost me my droid, my speeder, and almost my neck, Tig Fromm.|Boba Fett|<ref name="ARttF" />}}
Originally a standard zZip X-X landspeeder, the ''Silver Speeder'' was owned by an [[Unidentified Iotran|Iotran]].<ref name="P170" /> At some point between [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]<ref name="Date">"[[So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in ''Star Wars'', Part 1]]" states that [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] collected his first [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]] at the age of eleven, one year after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Jango Fett/Legends|his father]] at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|outbreak]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]].</ref> and [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],<ref name="15BBY">"[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]].</ref> the bounty hunter Boba Fett was [[Bounty/Legends|hired]] to hunt down the [[Iotran/Legends|Iotran]]. Fett had a difficult time catching his target after locating her on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rivvidu]] due to the power of the ''Silver Speeder''. Impressed by the vehicle, Fett decided to keep it. Fett had the landspeeder repainted to make it appear inconspicuous<ref name="P170" /> and outfitted it with nonstandard components, including weapons to assist him in his work.<ref name="CSWE" />

[[File:SomethingOnTheWindow-ARttF.png|thumb|right|250px|Boba Fett prepares to eject after a thermal detonator attaches to ''Silver Speeder''.]]
In 15 BBY,<ref name="15BBY" /> Fett was approached by [[Sise Fromm]], the leader of the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] criminal organization, to capture the [[Annoo resistance]] member [[Kea Moll]] and her friends, the speeder racers [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]],<ref name="ARttF" /> after the group destroyed Fromm's [[Starship/Legends|weapons satellite]], the ''[[Trigon One]]'', and his [[Sise Fromm's stronghold|stronghold]] on the planet [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]].<ref name="TTU">{{DroidsCite|The Trigon Unleashed}}</ref> Although Fromm's rival, [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], had put a bounty on Fromm, Fett owed the [[Annoo-dat Blue|Annoo-dat]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] a favor and accepted the contract. Fett took the ''Silver Speeder'' to the planet [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]], where Joben and Dusat were due to enter their racing landspeeder, the ''[[White Witch]]'', in the [[Boonta Speeder Race]]. The bounty hunter's first attempt to capture his targets failed;<ref name="ARttF" /> Fett's [[BL-series Battle Legionnaire]], [[BL-17]],<ref name="NEGD">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> lured Joben into a trap, but the intervention of the [[Monster droid/Legends|junk droid]] [[Proto One]] allowed Joben to escape in the ''White Witch''.<ref name="ARttF" />

Fett gave chase in the ''Silver Speeder'', following the ''White Witch'' even as Joben headed into the Boonta Speeder Race. The bounty hunter attacked Joben throughout the race, unaware that Sise Fromm's [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Tig Fromm]], had concealed a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] on the ''White Witch'' and set it to detonate at the end of the tenth and final lap. Fett first used the ''Silver Speeder'' to ram the ''White Witch'' in an attempt to make it crash. When this failed, he used his speeder's grappling cables to grab hold of the ''White Witch'', but Joben's co-pilot, the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], cut the cables, allowing Joben to break free. Fett then used his laser cannons to blast a hole in the ''White Witch''{{'s}} fuselage, causing it to vent smoke, but Joben managed to retain control while R2-D2 sealed the leak. As the race neared its end, Fett tried once more to stop Joben, using the ''Silver Speeder''{{'s}} magnetic beams to grab hold of the ''White Witch''. However, the magnetic beams inadvertently dislodged the thermal detonator, which became attached to the ''Silver Speeder''. Fett used his [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] to escape the doomed speeder, moments before it exploded as it crossed the finish line in second place behind Joben. Furious that Tig Fromm's interference had led to the destruction of the ''Silver Speeder'', Fett apprehended both Sise and Tig Fromm after the race with the intention of turning them over to Jabba the Hutt.<ref name="ARttF" />

==Owners and operators==
[[File:BobaFettM.jpg|thumb|195px|Boba Fett]]
During the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the ''Silver Speeder'' was owned by the bounty hunter Boba Fett.<ref name="ARttF" /> A [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] bounty hunter [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], Boba Fett was orphaned when his father was [[Death/Legends|killed]] at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|beginning]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in 22 BBY. During the Clone Wars, the young Fett followed in his father's footsteps by becoming a bounty hunter himself<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> and, by 15 BBY,<ref name="15BBY" /> he had gained a fearsome reputation.<ref name="ARttF" /> Fett acquired the ''Silver Speeder'' on the planet Rivvidu, taking it as a prize after successfully collecting a bounty on the speeder's previous owner, an Iotran.<ref name="P170" /> Fett subsequently modified the speeder with several weapons to assist in his future hunts.<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Silver Speeder'' was created for the [[Nelvana]] animated television series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']], making its only appearance in the fourth episode, "[[A Race to the Finish]],"<ref name="ARttF" /> written by [[Peter Sauder]] and [[Steven Wright]] and first broadcast on [[September 28]], [[1985]],<ref name="Insider27">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref>. The speeder was also depicted in the [[1987]] [[A Race to the Finish (book)|picture-book adaptation]] of the episode<ref name="ARttF-book">[[A Race to the Finish (book)|''A Race to the Finish'' picture book]]</ref> and later received entries in the [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded|second]] and [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded|third]] editions of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'', the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" /><ref name="AGttSW2">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref><ref name="AGttSWU3">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref><ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> The [[2004]] ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' article "''[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]''" by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] expanded on the speeder's history and provided [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] statistics.<ref name="P170" /> Artist [[Jeffery Carlisle|Jeff Carlisle]] based his illustrations for the article on the television show, but had some trouble drawing the ''Silver Speeder'' when he discovered it was never shown in its entirety in the episode.<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/jeff_carlisle_droids_revisited/index.html|text=''Droids'' Revisited: An Interview with Artist Jeff Carlisle}}</ref>
